description: Fix two bugs in the MCP server: a browser tab leak in analyze-site when openTab returns a malformed result, and a cross-platform path splitting issue in removeLocalPlugin.

userStories.title: Fix tab leak in analyze-site when openTab returns unexpected result
userStories.description: As a user running `plugin_analyze_site`, I want the tool to always clean up the browser tab it opens, even when the extension returns an unexpected result shape, so that orphan tabs do not accumulate.
acceptanceCriteria: The openTab call and its result validation are inside the try block that has the finally cleanup handler
If openTab succeeds but returns an unexpected shape (e.g., non-number id), the opened tab is still closed via the finally block
If openTab fails (throws), no tab cleanup is needed and the error propagates correctly
All existing analyze-site functionality continues to work (network capture enable/disable, navigation, script execution, tab close on success and error)

userStories.title: Use path.basename() instead of split('/') in removeLocalPlugin
userStories.description: As a user running plugin management on any platform, I want `removeLocalPlugin` to correctly extract the directory name from a resolved path so that plugin removal by directory name works on all operating systems.
acceptanceCriteria: The `removeLocalPlugin` function uses `basename()` from `node:path` instead of `resolvedPath.split('/').pop()`
Plugin removal by directory name matching works correctly (the dirName comparison logic is unchanged, only the extraction method changes)
userStories.notes:
Root cause: In `platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts` line 426, the directory name is extracted with `resolvedPath.split('/').pop()`. On Windows, `resolve()` produces paths with backslashes (e.g., `C:\Users\foo\plugin`), so `split('/')` would not split correctly — `pop()` would return the entire path string instead of just the directory name. This means the directory name matching on lines 429-432 would never match on Windows.

Fix: Import `basename` from `node:path` (which is already imported in the file — check the imports at the top) and replace `resolvedPath.split('/').pop() ?? ''` with `basename(resolvedPath)`. The `basename` function handles both forward and backslash separators on all platforms.

File to modify: `platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts` — line 426

Check the existing imports at the top of the file. If `basename` is not yet imported from `node:path`, add it to the existing import statement. If `path` functions are already imported (e.g., `resolve`, `join`), just add `basename` to that import.

This is a one-line fix: change `const dirName = resolvedPath.split('/').pop() ?? '';` to `const dirName = basename(resolvedPath);`
